sections.formula: All A are B (A inside B). Some A are B (Intersection).

examples.question: All Cats are Dogs. All Dogs are Birds. Cats are Birds?
examples.solution:
Step 1: Cats inside Dogs inside Birds.
Step 2: Yes, Cats are inside Birds.

sections.formula: If definite Conclusion is False, Possibility MIGHT be True.
sections.solvingTechnique: Try to draw a counter-diagram. If you can, possibility exists.

examples.question: Some A are B. No B is C. Can A be C?
examples.solution:
Step 1: A overlaps B. B separate from C.
Step 2: Part of A could overlap C.
Step 3: Yes, possible.

shortcuts: Restatement is NOT a valid conclusion.
Either-Or condition: Elements same, one +ve, one -ve, both invalid individually.
commonMistakes: Thinking 'Some' means 'Some implies not All'. (Some includes All).
Using real world logic (Cats aren't Dogs!).
placementTips: Master the 'Only a few' concept (means Some AND Some Not).
